$comment_1: Copy this file to team-<name>.json and fill it in. NEVER paste raw tokens — every secret MUST be an env-var placeholder of the form ${MY_ENV_VAR}. A CI check and the loader itself reject raw ghp_/github_pat_/sk- strings before they can reach runtime.
$comment_repos: One entry per repo this tenant can ask questions about. The loader resolves githubToken from env at startup.
$comment_issueConfig: Optional. Omit the whole block if this tenant does not draft issues. writeToken needs issues:write scope on targetRepo only.
$comment_ai: provider = claude | openai | gemini. apiKey is the provider key, not a GitHub token. dailySpendCapUsd is enforced per-tenant inside the provider impl.
systemPrompt: You are a support assistant for <product>. Describe the repo's intent here, what the assistant should and should not do, and any conventions worth highlighting. Always reference file paths when citing code.
$comment_security: allowedOrigins drives the per-tenant CORS check in production mode. allowedUsers is the current prototype gate; switch to OAuth in production.
$comment_defaultRepoScope: Optional. Owner/name identifiers the assistant may reason about by default when the request does not supply its own repoScope. Every entry must match owner/name AND reference a repo declared above — validated at load time.
